ops.title: Claude Code sessions steal fleet plugins into project scope (installed_plugins.json churn); yasyf-home healed via launchd scope-guard 2026-07-13; plugin list shows project-scoped entries as enabled even where they don't load

ops.body:
Claude Code (observed on 2.1.207) intermittently rewrites `~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json` install records for plugins from autoUpdate marketplaces that repos enable in project-level `enabledPlugins` (the cc-guides fleet stamp): a tool/skill-using session REPLACES the entry with `scope: project, projectPath: ` — the array does not append. Whichever repo last used them owns them; every other repo's sessions go dark (`agent type 'codex:codex-wrapper' not found`, `Unknown skill: codex`). Trivial `-p "hi"` sessions don't trigger it; steals are intermittent and correlate with plugin use. Diagnosis trap: `claude plugin list` shows a project-scoped plugin as "enabled" from any cwd — enablement ≠ installation; the loader only loads a project-scoped plugin when cwd matches its projectPath. **yasyf-home fix (2026-07-13):** all six fleet plugins (codex/slop-cop/llm-prompts/writing-docs @skills, cc-notes@cc-notes, captain-hook@captain-hook) re-scoped to user via CLI uninstall/install, plus a launchd guard `com.yasyf.claude-plugin-scope-guard` (WatchPaths + 300s interval) running `~/.claude/plugins/scope-guard/pin-scope-user.py`, which re-asserts a user-scope entry in place (r+ edit preserves the cc-pool 11-way hardlink fan-out — never write via temp+rename). Heal log: `~/.claude/plugins/scope-guard/scope-guard.log`. Remove with `launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.yasyf.claude-plugin-scope-guard.plist`. Local machine (same version/flags) appends instead of replacing — user entries persist here; the guard is only deployed on yasyf-home. **Update 2026-07-22 (audit-verified):** yBook-Pro is NOT immune — installed_plugins.json carried 7 project-scope records pinned to the captain-hook cwd, and slop-cop@skills + cc-guides@cc-guides were project-ONLY (dark from every other cwd) until a one-time `claude plugin install

@ --scope user` heal. The guard is confirmed absent here (no plist, no launchctl entry, no source in ~/Code — it lives only on yasyf-home). Productization + both-machine deploy is backlog task 4e3cfc03 in the cc-skills cc-notes store. Related: [[cc-pool-config-copies]], [[cc-guides-v3-architecture]] (repo-level enabledPlugins stamp is what exposes the churn), [[model-routing-convention]] (codex lane depends on this plugin loading). **Mechanism (claude-code-guide research, 2026-07-13):** documented autoUpdate reconciliation runs on every session start from any project (changelog v2.1.108 "policy-managed plugins never auto-updating when running from a different project"; docs discover-plugins §6.3, §7 "applies on every path that loads plugins" since v2.1.195) × a known registry defect where the passive reconciliation path treats a plugin's install record as a global singleton keyed by name (issue cluster #27257/#26513/#20390/#30263/#29997/#27800 fixed only for the interactive install path; **#14202 still open**). Per-repo `enabledPlugins`+`extraKnownMarketplaces` (the cc-guides fleet stamp) makes every repo's session a stakeholder re-asserting the slot. Doc-sanctioned arrangement for one person/machine = enablement in **user settings only** (discover-plugins §4); the per-repo blocks are the team-onboarding mechanism (plugin-marketplaces §7.5). `autoUpdate:false` in known_marketplaces.json never sticks — settings extraKnownMarketplaces re-assert it (changelog v2.1.141). `-s local` hits the same singleton path (#26513). Exact upstream match: **claude-code#75392** (open, unfixed as of 2026-07-13) — plugin registration writes don't read-merge-write; since v2.1.195 that path fires implicitly on every plugin-load path (docs discover-plugins §7), so a tool/skill-using session silently re-registers every project-enabled plugin in one non-merging pass. No supported scope-pin exists (#62209 `pluginDefaultScope` closed stale, unimplemented) — an external guard is the only mitigation. Our repro (implicit trigger via skill invocation, scope-clobber without wiping unrelated entries) is a materially wider trigger than #75392's filed CLI repro — worth commenting there.
